'''大山神社'''という名称の神社には以下のものがある。 * [[大山神社 (富加町)]] - 岐阜県加茂郡富加町にある神社 * [[大山神社 (尾道市)]] - 広島県尾道市因島土生町にある神社 * [[大山神社 (西ノ島町)]] - 島根県隠岐郡西ノ島町にある神社 {{aimai}}

'''大山神社'''は[[神社]]。
* [[大山神社 (富加町)]] - 岐阜県加茂郡富加町鎮座 * [[大山神社 (尾道市)]] - 広島県尾道市因島土生町鎮座 * [[大山神社 (西ノ島町)]] - 島根県隠岐郡西ノ島町鎮座
{{曖昧さ回避}} {{デフォルトソート:おおやましんしや}} [[Category:同名の神社]]

revision.comment: Created by translating the page "[[:en:Special:Redirect/revision/1148724875|Draft:Oyama Shrine (Mount Tate)]]"
revision.text:
{| class="infobox vcard" style="border-spacing:2px;" ! colspan="2" class="infobox-above" style="background-color: #D3D3D3" |Oyama Shrine |- | colspan="2" class="infobox-image" |[[File:20_Tateyama_from_Mikurigaike_1998-7-17.jpg|frameless]] |- ! colspan="2" class="infobox-header" style="background-color: #D3D3D3" |Religion |- class="note" ! class="infobox-label" scope="row" |[[List of religions and spiritual traditions|Affiliation]] | class="infobox-data" |Shinto |- ! class="infobox-label" scope="row" |[[Deity]] | class="infobox-data" |[[Ame-no-Tajikarao]], [[Izanagi]] |}
Oyama Shrine (雄山神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Japan that worships [[Mount Tate]]. The shrine is located at a high altitude of 3,003 meters and has three sub-shrines.<ref>https://zekkeijapan.com/spot/index/1147/</ref>
Oyama Shrine has a rich history and was historically a major site of Shugendo, a Japanese religion that combines elements of Buddhism, Shintoism, and other indigenous beliefs.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Oyama Jinja - Toyama|url=https://en.japantravel.com/toyama/oyama-jinja/2595|access-date=2023-04-07|website=JapanTravel|language=en}}</ref>
The shrine claims to be the Ichinomiya (highest ranked Shinto shrine) of [[Etchū Province]], but there are other shrines that also claim this title.
Oyama Shrine is unique in that it does not have a honden, which is a main hall for enshrining the kami (deities), instead, [[Mount Tate]] itself is considered its main object of worship, known as the [[kannabi]].
== See Also ==
* Other shrines claiming to be Ecchu Ichinomiya ** Imizu Jinja ** Keta Jinja (Takaoka) ** Takase Shrine
== References == {{Reflist}}{{Draft categories|[[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Etchū Province]] [[Category:Hida Mountains]] [[Category:Mountain faith]]}}

== References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Mountain faith]]

{| class="infobox vcard" style="border-spacing:2px;" ! colspan="2" | Oyama Shrine |- | colspan="2" class="infobox-image" |[[File:20_Tateyama_from_Mikurigaike_1998-7-17.jpg|frameless]] |- class="note" ! class="infobox-label" scope="row" |[[List of religions and spiritual traditions|Affiliation]] | class="infobox-data" |[[Shinto]] |- ! class="infobox-label" scope="row" |[[Deity]] | class="infobox-data" |{{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|en}}, [[Izanagi]] |}
Oyama Shrine (雄山神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Japan that worships [[Mount Tate]]. The shrine is located at a high altitude of 3,003 meters and has three sub-shrines.<ref>https://zekkeijapan.com/spot/index/1147/</ref>
Oyama Shrine has a rich history and was historically a major site of {{ill|Shugendo|en}}, a Japanese religion that combines elements of Buddhism, Shintoism, and other indigenous beliefs.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Oyama Jinja - Toyama|url=https://en.japantravel.com/toyama/oyama-jinja/2595|access-date=2023-04-07|website=JapanTravel|language=en}}</ref>
The shrine claims to be the Ichinomiya (highest ranked Shinto shrine) of [[Etchū Province]], but there are other shrines that also claim this title.
Oyama Shrine is unique in that it does not have a honden, which is a main hall for enshrining the kami (deities), instead, [[Mount Tate]] itself is considered its main object of worship, known as the [[kannabi]].
== See Also ==
* Other shrines claiming to be [[Etchū Province|Etchū]] Ichinomiya ** {{ill|Imizu Jinja|en}} ** {{ill|Keta Jinja (Takaoka)|lt=Keta Jinja|en}} ** {{ill|Takase Shrine|en}}
== References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Mountain faith]]
